category_title: Students with CHARGE syndrome exhibiting challenging behaviors (kicking, biting, scratching) to escape tasks or due to lack of structure
minimal_context: David is in the classroom presented with a table-top object sorting activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: (Places a pile of mixed objects on the table) It is time to sort the objects.

STUDENT: (Grinds his teeth and engages in visual self-stimulatory behavior instead of starting the task)

TUTOR: (Points to the objects) Keep trying, put them in the right spots.

STUDENT: (Stands up from the table and begins jumping)

TUTOR: (Taps the table) Sit down and finish your table-top activity.

STUDENT: (Scratches the tutor's arm and kicks the table, exhibiting problematic behaviors to escape the activity)

options.A: Presents single letters or shapes within his intact visual field with good contrast and no visual clutter.
options.B: Provides worksheets with dotted letters or shapes on them for the student during the table-top activity.
options.C: Checks the student's progress on the sorting activity every Sunday night to identify mistakes and areas of struggle.
options.D: Blurs boundaries between table-top activities to focus on integrated themes and co-construct a shared sense of morality.
